narrative: The FY2023-24 benchmark shows clear improvement across most companies but no convergence for Tata. SAIL and Jindal led employee LTIFR with 0 and 0.04, JSW led worker LTIFR at 0.09, and Jindal led TRIFR for both employees and workers. Tata improved on employee LTIFR, worker LTIFR, employee TRIFR and worker TRIFR, but remained 4th on all four core frequency/recordable indicators. Fatality performance improved for Tata employees, with zero employee fatalities, but worker fatalities remained elevated at 5. Jindal’s safety system is notable: ISO 45001:2018, 4-E principle, safety culture, health surveillance and explicit accident-free steel ambition. Tata’s FY24 disclosure also shows systematic corrective-action and family-support mechanisms, but the peer benchmark indicates that governance maturity had not yet translated into top-quartile incident outcomes. The operational implication for Tata is to focus less on system presence and more on verified critical-control effectiveness at contractor and high-energy interfaces.
